title: Perforated eardrum follow-up
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.
examination.rationale: The exact adult hearing section supports interval hearing, pain, discharge, tinnitus, vertigo, functional impact, relevant negatives, clinician-performed ENT examination, and clinician concern when tympanic-membrane abnormality is present; it does not determine healing or cause.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.

source.title: Hearing loss in adults: assessment and management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.5–1.3.2 — additional symptoms, ear findings, and referral

investigations.text: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.
investigations.rationale: The exact audiology section supports concerns, reviewed audiology or tympanometry, specialist information, entered plan, safety net, follow-up, and questions without recommending water precautions, drops, surgery, imaging, or referral.
investigations.evidence_extract: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.

source_limitations: Age, cause and date, laterality, perforation site and size, pain, discharge, hearing, vertigo, examination, audiology, healing, infection, treatment, procedure, and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
